@graph.name: Door Installation & Replacement - Capitol Improvements
@graph.description: Provia Door & Window Certified contractor installing fiberglass, steel, and custom entry doors in Maryland, DC, and Virginia. Storm doors, patio doors, and French doors available.

itemOffered.name: Fiberglass Entry Door Replacement
itemOffered.description: Provia fiberglass doors — durable, low-maintenance, available in wood-grain finishes. Polyurethane foam core.

itemOffered.name: Steel Entry Door Replacement
itemOffered.description: Heavy-gauge steel entry doors for maximum security. Provia Signet and Heritage lines with multi-point locking.

itemOffered.name: Storm Door Installation
itemOffered.description: Full-view and self-storing storm doors that protect your entry door and add insulation.

itemOffered.name: Sliding Patio Door Replacement
itemOffered.description: Sliding glass patio doors with multi-point locking and Low-E glass for energy efficiency.

itemOffered.name: French Door Installation
itemOffered.description: Double French door systems for patios and rear entries. Available in fiberglass and steel.

itemOffered.name: Custom Milled Door Installation
itemOffered.description: Custom wood and specialty doors for historic homes and unique openings.

mainEntity.name: What types of doors do you install?

acceptedAnswer.text: We install fiberglass entry doors, steel entry doors, storm doors, sliding patio doors, French doors, and custom milled wood doors. We primarily work with Provia.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between fiberglass and steel entry doors?

acceptedAnswer.text: Steel doors offer superior security and are typically less expensive. Fiberglass doors resist denting, don’t rust, and can mimic real wood grain. Both have energy-efficient foam cores.
Yes. Provia doors feature polyurethane foam cores, compression weatherstripping, and Low-E glass options. Many meet or exceed ENERGY STAR requirements.

mainEntity.name: How long does door installation take?

acceptedAnswer.text: A single entry door replacement typically takes 2–4 hours. Full systems with sidelites or transoms may take a full day.
